text.p: parts of North East Pastoral district.
The State Emergency Service advises that people should:
* Don't drive, ride or walk through flood water;
* Keep clear of creeks and storm drains;
* Stay indoors, away from windows, while conditions are severe.

warning-info.text: The next Severe Weather Warning will be issued by 11:00 am ACDT Wednesday.

text.p: Check https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts. Warnings are also available through TV and Radio broadcasts or call 1300 659 210. The Bureau and State Emergency Service would appreciate warnings being broadcast regularly.

forecast-period.text: HEAVY RAINFALL ACROSS THE NORTH EAST PASTORAL.

text.p: Weather Situation: A rich tropical airmass extends over the centre of the country with a surface trough extending through northeast parts of the state. Meanwhile an upper trough is moving across eastern parts of SA. This combination is continuing to bring the risk of heavy rainfall to northeastern parts of the state today.
HEAVY RAINFALL which may lead to FLASH FLOODING is forecast for parts of the North East Pastoral district today. Six-hourly rainfall totals between 50 and 80 mm are likely.
INTENSE RAINFALL is no longer expected, with broader conditions expected to ease during this afternoon.
Flood Watches and Warnings are current for the area. Please refer to https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts/ for more information.
Locations which may be affected include Moomba, Innamincka, Arkaroola, the area east of Lake Frome, Mungeranie on the Birdsville Track and the Strzelecki Track south of Moomba.
HEAVY RAINFALL which may lead to FLASH FLOODING is forecast for parts of the North East Pastoral district today. Six-hourly rainfall totals between 50 and 80 mm are likely.
INTENSE RAINFALL is no longer expected, with broader conditions expected to ease during this afternoon.
Flood Watches and Warnings are current for the area. Please refer to https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts/ for more information.
Locations which may be affected include Moomba, Innamincka, Arkaroola, the area east of Lake Frome, Mungeranie on the Birdsville Track and the Strzelecki Track south of Moomba.
